ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Banh Mi Bo Xao; closest parent is beef sandwich food product (FOODON_00002473).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers sandwiches with meat filling; no specific code for Vietnamese-style beef banh mi exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this specific dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard banh mi sandwich roll filled with sauteed beef and white onion, approximately 220 g, as typically served at a Vietnamese-style restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beef sandwich with vegetables on roll (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); supplemented with USDA SR Legacy entries for sauteed beef, mayonnaise, and baguette components
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Banh Mi Bo Xao exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite for beef sandwich with vegetables on roll (FDC ID 172136) adjusted for the specific components described: sauteed beef (~35%), baguette (~30%), white onion (~10%), mayonnaise (~8%), pickled vegetables (~8%), cilantro (~2%), soy sauce (~4%), chilli sauce (~3%). Macros cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked beef (FDC ID 174032) and French baguette (FDC ID 175023).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread / baguette-based sandwich with protein and fat filling
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Banh Mi Bo Xao. GI estimated at 55 (medium) based on the analogous food category of white baguette-based sandwich with protein and fat filling. White baguette alone has GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database); the addition of sauteed beef, mayonnaise, pickled vegetables, and chilli sauce substantially lowers the effective GI of the composite meal to approximately 55, consistent with published mixed-meal GI attenuation data (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2021). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 x 45.1) / 100 = 24.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce, chilli sauce, and pickled vegetables
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sandwich due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 520 mg per 100 g (approximately 1144 mg per serving), which represents over 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced soy sauce and omitting pickled vegetables can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from white baguette carbohydrates, partially offset by protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that this sandwich has an estimated glycemic load of approximately 24.8 per serving, placing it in the moderate-to-high GL range; portion control is advised, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables or choosing a smaller roll can help attenuate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef and mayonnaise, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this sandwich in moderation given its saturated fat content of approximately 2.9 g per 100 g and high sodium load; choosing leaner beef cuts and reducing mayonnaise quantity would improve the cardiovascular risk profile of this dish.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Banh Mi Bo Xao (Bánh Mì Bò Xào) is a Vietnamese-style sauteed beef sandwich served on a crispy French baguette roll, popular in Singapore at Vietnamese restaurants such as Co Chung Restaurant. The filling consists of thinly sliced beef stir-fried with white onion in a savoury sauce, layered with homemade mayonnaise, soy sauce, pickled daikon and carrot, fresh cilantro, and chilli sauce. The baguette provides a light, airy crumb with a crisp crust, while the sauteed beef delivers a savoury, slightly caramelised flavour with tender texture. The combination of pickled vegetables and fresh herbs gives the sandwich its characteristic Vietnamese flavour profile — a balance of savoury, tangy, and spicy notes. Nutritionally, this sandwich is a moderate-energy meal providing approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from beef (12.5 g/100 g) and carbohydrates primarily from the baguette (20.5 g/100 g). The sodium content is notably high due to soy sauce and pickled components, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 24.8, relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. This dish reflects the Vietnamese culinary tradition of banh mi — a fusion of French colonial baguette culture with Southeast Asian flavours — adapted for the Singapore food service market.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Banh Mi Bo Xao exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional nutrition database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data for beef sandwiches with vegetables on a roll, adjusted for the specific ingredient proportions described in the field survey listing. GI is an analogous estimate based on white baguette sandwich with protein and fat filling from the University of Sydney GI Database framework. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source composite for this food type. All other micronutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the component ingredients. Sodium content is notably high due to soy sauce, pickled vegetables, and chilli sauce; actual sodium may vary significantly by restaurant preparation. The halal status is listed as false because it cannot be confirmed from the field survey listing alone — beef sourcing and preparation method at Co Chung Restaurant have not been independently verified for halal certification. Price data (SGD 20.58) is preserved from the input record for reference only and is not used in nutritional calculations. This record was generated from a field survey data seed record and requires verification against a primary nutrition database if one becomes available for this specific dish.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros present and numeric: energy_kcal 218.0, protein_g 12.5, fat_g 9.8, carbohydrate_g 20.5. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite with named FDC IDs and adaptation methodology documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 1.4, sodium_mg 520.0, saturated_fat_g 2.9, sugar_g 3.2 — all present as numeric values and plausible for a beef sandwich with soy sauce and pickled vegetables.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 55 (numeric, greater than zero), gi_category medium, glycemic_load_per_serving 24.8, gi_source documented with University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 as analogous estimate basis.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (beef sandwich food product) used as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A0FKP (sandwich with meat filling) used as closest_parent with gap note. SNOMED CT set to none with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is approximately 1350 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern, glycemic load), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, saturated fat and sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is Type 2 Diabetes.

All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
